channel.title: World - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Live updates: Ceasefire disintegrates as Trump says US will reinstate blockade of Iranian ships in Strait of Hormuz - CNN
item.description:
1. Live updates: Ceasefire disintegrates as Trump says US will reinstate blockade of Iranian ships in Strait of Hormuz  CNN
  2. Trump offers US protection in the Strait of Hormuz for a 20% fee. How would that work?  CNN
  3. Oil jumps as Trump claims new US blockade on Iran in Strait of Hormuz  Axios
  4. Trump proposes 20% toll on cargo through Strait of Hormuz; restarts Iran blockade  CNBC
  5. Trump’s Threat to Impose Fees in the Strait of Hormuz Contradicts His Aides  The New York Times

item.title: America’s Latest Weapon Against Iran: Sea Drones - WSJ
item.description:
1. America’s Latest Weapon Against Iran: Sea Drones  WSJ
  2. Trump orders new round of strikes to "hold Iranian forces accountable," CENTCOM says as two sides exchange threats  CBS News
  3. The US Navy's new sea drones have gone from rescuing downed airmen to blowing up Iranian targets  Business Insider
  4. In a First, U.S. Forces Used Naval Drones in Combat Operations  The New York Times
  5. U.S. and Iran exchange strikes as Strait of Hormuz standoff escalates  CNBC

item.title: Ukraine, 9 nations form ballistic missile defense coalition - dw.com
item.description:
1. Ukraine, 9 nations form ballistic missile defense coalition  dw.com

  4. Ukraine to produce French missiles, orders war planes, Macron says  Reuters
  5. Ukraine and allies set up coalition to tackle Russia ballistic missile threat  NBC News

item.title: Children's social media curbs planned across EU, von der Leyen says - Reuters
item.description:
1. Children's social media curbs planned across EU, von der Leyen says  Reuters

  3. Von der Leyen convinced by case for age restrictions on social media  Euronews.com
  4. EU Proposal to Curb Minors’ Access to Social Media Coming Later This Year  WSJ
  5. EU chief pledges social media ban to protect children from ‘predatory algorithms’  The Guardian

item.title: British counterterrorism police take over investigation into "horrific murder" of veteran politician - CBS News
item.description:
1. British counterterrorism police take over investigation into "horrific murder" of veteran politician  CBS News
  2. Ann Widdecombe suspect not known to Prevent as counter terrorism police take over investigation  BBC
  3. MPs pay fitting tribute to Widdecombe, even if she would have complained about the ‘fuss’  The Telegraph
  4. Mahmood offers Farage security meeting after Ann Widdecombe’s death  The Guardian
  5. Suspect in former U.K. minister’s killing rearrested on suspicion of terror  NBC News

item.title: EU and Britain target Russian intelligence officers over a major cyberspying campaign - AP News
item.description:
1. EU and Britain target Russian intelligence officers over a major cyberspying campaign  AP News
  2. Russian Cyberattacks Stretching Back 15 Years Prompt European Sanctions  WSJ
  3. Human rights violations in Russia: Council lists four individuals and five entities over abusive surveillance  consilium.europa.eu
  4. France set to summon Russian ambassador to Paris over alleged cyberattack campaign  Reuters
  5. NSA and Partners Release Guidance on Improving Router Hygiene to Protect Against Russian S  National Security Agency (NSA) (.gov)

item.title: Fire at Bangkok bar kills 27; exits may have been blocked, officials say - The Washington Post
item.description:
1. Fire at Bangkok bar kills 27; exits may have been blocked, officials say  The Washington Post

  3. Video shows huge fire at a Bangkok beer hall that killed 27  AP News
  4. Fire breaks out at a pub in Bangkok, killing at least 27 people, officials say  NBC News
  5. At least 27 people dead after fire breaks out at in Bangkok pub  PBS

item.title: Yemen government forces attack Sanaa airport, intercept missiles - Al Jazeera
item.description:
1. Yemen government forces attack Sanaa airport, intercept missiles  Al Jazeera

  4. Saudi says it intercepted Houthi missiles fired at south in test of years-long truce  Reuters

item.title: Inside Israel’s Secret Operation to Cultivate Ahmadinejad - The New York Times
item.description:
1. Inside Israel’s Secret Operation to Cultivate Ahmadinejad  The New York Times
  2. Israel courted Iran’s former hardline president for post-regime role, reports claim  The Guardian

  4. Former Iranian president Ahmadinejad under arrest by IRGC for work with Mossad  The Jerusalem Post
  5. Inside the Mossad Plot to Install Israel's Arch-enemy Ahmadinejad as Iran's Leader  Haaretz

item.title: Wildfire rages near Paris as heatwave scorches Europe - Reuters
item.description:
1. Wildfire rages near Paris as heatwave scorches Europe  Reuters

  4. Planes scoop up water from River Seine in effort to battle wildfire near Paris  CNN
  5. Wildfire of "exceptional scale" burning near Paris  CBS News

item.title: U.K. says an Iran-backed group was behind attacks on Jewish community, bans Revolutionary Guard - NBC News
item.description:
1. U.K. says an Iran-backed group was behind attacks on Jewish community, bans Revolutionary Guard  NBC News

item.title: Briton tells of surviving Spain wildfire in car as wife and friends died trying to walk to safety - BBC
item.description:
1. Briton tells of surviving Spain wildfire in car as wife and friends died trying to walk to safety  BBC

  4. Son of Belgian wildfire victim disputes Spanish officials' account of warnings  Reuters

item.title: Japan is building a new intelligence agency with help from the West - The Japan Times
item.description:
1. Japan is building a new intelligence agency with help from the West  The Japan Times
  2. Japan Builds Intelligence Agency It Hasn’t Had Since World War II  The New York Times
  3. What is Japan’s new intelligence agency, and why is Tokyo building it?  Al Jazeera
  4. Decades Of Silence, Then $407 Million Answer: Japan Is Building A Spy Agency  NDTV
  5. $407m to end 'spy paradise': Japan enlists Western allies for first intelligence agency in 80 years  upday News

item.title: Thousands died in England and Wales from recent heat waves: Report - ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos
item.description:
1. Thousands died in England and Wales from recent heat waves: Report  ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos
  2. More than 2,700 people may have died in exceptional May and June heatwaves in England and Wales  BBC
  3. May and June heatwaves killed about 2,700 people in England and Wales, data suggests  The Guardian
  4. More than 2,700 deaths in UK linked to May, June heatwaves  Al Jazeera
  5. Fearmongering about heatwave deaths is foolish and unscientific  The Telegraph
item.source: ABC News - Breaking News, Latest News and Videos

item.title: Ukraine Strikes Russian Oil Depot Again as Drone Attacks Reach Moscow, Killing 3 - Kyiv Post
item.description:
1. Ukraine Strikes Russian Oil Depot Again as Drone Attacks Reach Moscow, Killing 3  Kyiv Post

  4. Ukrainian drones batter Russian oil facilities and set more oil tankers ablaze  AP News
  5. Ukraine strikes Russian ships near Crimea, escalating attacks on fuel supplies  BBC

item.title: Toronto police looking for suspects after deadly shooting at festival - NPR
item.description:

item.title: Former DEVGRU operator Matt Bissonnette sheds new light on how the bin Laden raid actually unfolded - Fox News

item.title: Sheikh Hamad: The Arab leader who broke Israel’s siege on Gaza - Al Jazeera
item.description:
1. Sheikh Hamad: The Arab leader who broke Israel’s siege on Gaza  Al Jazeera

  4. Former Emir of Qatar Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani dies aged 74  BBC
